---
title: "gtscales Typst Example"
format:
  typst: default
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
devtools::load_all(".")
library(gt)
```

This example shows the current Typst workflow for standalone legend rendering.

It includes both:

- a continuous legend rendered as a true gradient bar
- a stepped legend rendered as labeled swatches

## Continuous Gradient

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
continuous_spec <- gtscale_spec_continuous(
  num,
  palette = c("#fdd49e", "#fdbb84", "#ef6548", "#990000"),
  breaks = c(0, 18, 444, 8880000),
  title = "Continuous gradient"
)

continuous_legend <- gtscale_render_legend(
  spec = continuous_spec,
  data = gt::gt(gt::exibble),
  output = "typst"
)

cat("```{=typst}\n")
cat(continuous_legend)
cat("\n```\n")
```

## Stepped Quantiles

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
quantile_spec <- gtscale_spec_quantiles(
  num,
  palette = c("#fdd49e", "#fdbb84", "#ef6548", "#990000"),
  quantiles = 4,
  title = "Stepped quantiles"
)

quantile_legend <- gtscale_render_legend(
  spec = quantile_spec,
  data = gt::gt(gt::exibble),
  output = "typst"
)

cat("```{=typst}\n")
cat(quantile_legend)
cat("\n```\n")
```
